description: Soy-honey marinated chicken skewers threaded with pineapple and caramelized for a sweet, smoky grilled dinner.

ingredients.note: peeled, cored, and cut into chunks

ingredients.item: rice vinegar or apple cider vinegar

steps: In a bowl, combine the soy sauce, honey, vinegar, teriyaki sauce (if using), garlic powder, ginger, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Add the chicken cubes and toss to coat. Marinate in the fridge for at least 20 minutes (up to 1 hour).
Preheat the oven to 425°F (220°C) or heat a grill pan or skillet over medium-high heat. If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for 15-20 minutes to prevent burning.
Thread the chicken cubes and pineapple chunks onto the skewers, alternating between the chicken and pineapple.
Heat the olive oil in a pan. Once hot, add the pineapple chunks and cook for 2-3 minutes, flipping to caramelize the sides.

Place the skewers on a parchment- or foil-lined baking sheet, brush with a bit of the remaining marinade, and bake for 20-25 minutes, flipping halfway through, until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C).
Brush the cooked skewers with extra teriyaki glaze if desired and serve immediately.
